passages.title: Australian parrots
passages.source_html:
Australian parrots


and their adaptation to habitat change


A


Parrots are found across the tropics and in all southern hemisphere continents except Antarctica, but nowhere do they display such a richness of diversity and form as in Australia. One-sixth of the world's 345 parrot species are found there, and Australia has long been renowned for the number and variety of its parrots.


B


In the 16th century, the German cartographer Mercator made a world map that included a place, somewhere near present-day Australia, that he named Terra Psittacorum – the Land of Parrots – and the first European settlers in Australia often referred to the country as Parrot Land. In 1865, the celebrated British naturalist and wildlife artist John Gould said: 'No group of birds gives Australia so tropical and benign an air as the numerous species of this great family by which it is tenanted.'


C


Parrots are descendants of an ancient line. Due to their great diversity, and since most species inhabit Africa, Australia and South America, it seems almost certain that parrots originated millions of years ago on the ancient southern continent of Gondwana, before it broke up into the separate southern hemisphere continents we know today. Much of Gondwana comprised vast rainforests intersected by huge slow-flowing rivers and expansive lakes, but by eight million years ago, great changes were underway. The center of the continent of Australia had begun to dry out, and the rainforests that once covered it gradually contracted to the continental margins, where, to a limited extent, they still exist today.


D


The creatures that remained in those shrinking rainforests had to adapt to the drier conditions or face extinction. Reacting to these desperate circumstances, the parrot family, typically found in jungles in other parts of the world, has populated some of Australia's harshest environments. The parrots spread from ancestral forests through eucalypt woodlands to colonies in the central deserts of Australia, and as a consequence they diversified into a wide range of species with adaptations that reflect the many changes animals and plants had to make to survive in these areas.


E


These evolutionary pressures helped mould keratin, the substance from which beaks are made into a range of tools capable of gathering the new food types favored by various species of parrot. The size of a parrot's short, blunt beak and the length of that beak’s downcurved upper section are related to the type of food each species eats. Some have comparatively long beaks that are perfect for extracting seeds from fruit; others have broader and stronger beaks that are designed for cracking hard seeds.


F


Differently shaped beaks are not the only adaptations that have been made during the developing relationship between parrots and their food plants. Like all of Australia's many honey-eating birds, the rainbow-coloured lorikeets and the flowers on which they feed have long co-evolved with features such as the shape and colour of the flowers adapted to the bird's particular needs, and physical adaptation of the birds designed to assist plants. For example, red is the most attractive colour to birds, and thus flowers which depend on birds for pollination are more often red, and lorikeets' tongues have bristles which help them to collect as much pollen as possible.


G


Today, most of Australia's parrots inhabit woodland and open forest, and their numbers decline towards both deserts and wetter areas. The majority are nomadic to some degree, moving around to take advantage of feeding and breeding places. Two of the dry country parrots, the pink and grey galah and the pink, white and yellow corella have expanded their ranges in recent years. They are among the species that have adapted well to the changes brought about by European settlement. Forest felling created grasslands where galahs and corellas thrive.


H


But other parrot species did not fare so well when their environments were altered. The clearing of large areas of rainforest is probably responsible for the disappearance of the double-eyed fig parrot, and numbers of ground parrots declined when a great part of their habitat was destroyed by the draining of coastal swamps. Even some parrot species that benefited from forest clearing at first are now confronted by a shortage of nesting sites due to further man-made changes.


I


New conditions also sometimes favour an incoming species over one that originally inhabited the area. For example, after farmers cleared large areas of forest on Kangaroo Island off the coast of South Australia, the island was colonised by galahs. They were soon going down holes and destroying black cockatoo eggs in order to take the hole for their own use. Their success precipitated a partial collapse in the black cockatoo population when the latter lost the struggle for scarce nesting hollows.


J


There may be no final answer to ensuring an equitable balance between parrot species. Nest box programmers help ease the shortage of nesting sites in some places, but there are not enough, they are expensive and they are not an adequate substitute by large, old trees, such as the habitat they represent and nectar, pollen and seeds they provide. Competition between parrots for nest sites is a result of the changes we humans have made to the Earth. We are the most widespread and dangerous competitors that parrots have ever had to face, but we also have the knowledge and skill to maintain the wonderfully rich diversity of Australia's parrots. All we need is the wild to do so.

groups.title: Questions 14-26
groups.source_html:
Questions 14 – 19


Look at the ten paragraphs (A – J). Which paragraph contains the following information?

You may choose any letter more than once.

Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
14

14 an example of how one parrot species may survive at the expense of another
✅ 正确答案：I

定位关键词：

survive at the expense of another

原文：

I段，“For example, after farmers cleared large areas of forest on Kangaroo Island off the coast of South Australia, the island was colonised by galahs. They were soon going down holes and destroying black cockatoo eggs in order to take the hole for their own use. Their success precipitated a partial collapse in the black cockatoo population when the later lost the struggle for scarce nesting hollows.”

解析：

题干信息是一个鹦鹉物种如何以牺牲另一个物种为代价而生存的例子，at the expense of这个词组可能会有理解不到位的情况。原文I段提到了galahs破坏黑凤头鹦鹉的蛋，以便把洞占为己用，这使黑风头鹦鹉的数量下降。所以此题干信息与I段对应。
15

15 a description of how plants may adapt to attract birds
✅ 正确答案：F

定位关键词：

plants；attract birds

原文：

F段，“For example, red is the most attractive colour to birds, and thus flowers which depend on birds for pollination are more often red

解析：

题干信息是植物如何适应以吸引鸟类，原文F段提到红色对鸟类来说是最具吸引力的颜色，因此依靠鸟类授粉的花朵通常是红色的，也就表明植物会通过开红色的话来吸引鸟类，此题干信息存在于F段。

同义替换：

the most attractive colour to birds=attract birds
16

16 example of two parrot species which benefited from changes to the environment
✅ 正确答案：G

定位关键词：

benefited from changes to the environment

原文：

G段，“Two of the dry country parrots, the pink and grey galah and the pink, white and yellow corella have expanded their ranges in recent years. They are among the species that have adapted well to the changes brought about by European settlement forest telling created grasslands where galahs and corellas thrive.”

解析：

题干信息是两种鹦鹉从环境变化中收益的例子，原文G段提到了两种乡村鹦鹉适应了欧洲的森林到草原的变化，此题干信息与G段对应。
17

17 how the varied Australian landscape resulted in a great variety of parrot species
✅ 正确答案：D

定位关键词：

landscape resulted in a great variety

原文：

D段，“The parrots spread from ancestral forests through eucalypt woodlands to colonies the central deserts of Australia, and as a consequence they diversified into a wide range of species"

解析：

题干信息为澳大利亚多变的地貌如何造就了种类繁多的鹦鹉物种，原文D段提到鹦鹉从原始森林通过桉树林地传播到澳大利亚中部沙漠的殖民地，因此它们多样化成各种各样的物种，这就表明是不同的地质地貌形成了不同的鹦鹉物种，此题信息在D段。

同义替换：

diversified into a wide range of species=a great variety
18

18 a reason why most parrot species are native to the southern hemisphere
✅ 正确答案：C

定位关键词：

native to the southern hemisphere

原文：

C段，“Parrots are descendants of an ancient line. Due to their great diversity, and since most species inhabit Africa, Australia and South America, it seems almost certain that parrots originated millions of years ago on the ancient southern continent of Gondwana, before it broke up into the separate southern hemisphere continents we know today."

解析：

题干信息是大多数鹦鹉都是在南半球（hemisphere）生长的原因，其中生词hemisphere意思可能不知道，但可以通过southern知道应该是南部的地方，且是原文原词。原文C段提到由于鹦鹉的多样性，而且大多数物种栖息在非洲、澳大利亚和南美洲，几乎可以肯定鹦鹉起源于数百万年前的古代南部冈瓦纳大陆，然后分裂成我们今天所知道的独立的南半球大陆，所以题干信息就在C段。其中A选项从非洲和南美搬到澳大利亚的信息未提到，B选项鹦鹉祖先应该生活在Gondwana南部，D选项的南半球大陆是不被水覆盖的，所以该三项都不正确。


同义替换：

southern hemisphere=southern continent
19

19 an example of a parrot species which did not survive changes to its habitat
✅ 正确答案：H

定位关键词：

not survive changes to its habitat

原文：

H段，“The clearing of large areas of rainforest is probably responsible for the disappearance of the double-eyed fig parrot.”


解析：

题干信息是没有在栖息地变化中存活下来的鹦鹉的例子，原文H段提到了双眼无花果鹦鹉(double-eyed fig parro)由于大面积雨林的砍伐而消失，地面鹦鹉的数量也因为沿海沼泽的排水破坏了大部分栖息地而减少。此题干信息与H段对应。

Questions 20 - 22


Choose the correct answer.

20

The writer believes that most parrot species

正确答案： C. had ancestors in a continent which later split up.

定位关键词：

most


原文：

C段，“Parrots are descendants of an ancient line. Due to their great diversity, and since most species inhabit Africa, Australia and South America, it seems almost certain that parrots originated millions of years ago on the ancient southern continent of Gondwana, before it broke up into the separate southern hemisphere continents we know today."


解析：

题干需要选择大多数鹦鹉是怎么样的，根据原文信息可知，鹦鹉是一个古老家族的后代，起源于数百万年前的古代南部冈瓦纳大陆，然后分裂成我们今天所知道的独立的南半球大陆，所以C选项祖先生活在后面分裂的大路上与原文相符，此题选择C。其中A选项从非洲和南美搬到澳大利亚的信息未提到，B选项鹦鹉祖先应该生活在Gondwana南部，D选项的南半球大陆是不被水覆盖的，所以该三项都不正确。


同义替换：

split up=break up

21

What does the writer say about parrot's beak?

正确答案： D. They are specially adapted to suit the diet.

定位关键词：

parrot's beak


原文：

E段，“The size of a parrot's short, blunt beak and the lengthof that beak’s downcurved upper section are related to the type of food each species eats."



解析：

原文提到鹦鹉短而钝的喙的大小和喙上部向下弯曲的长度与每个物种所吃的食物类型有关，和D选项They are specially adaptedto suit the diet.它们是经过特别调整以适应这种饮食的表述相符合。A、B、C三个选项在原文未提到。


同义替换：

suit the diet=are related to the type of food

22

Which of the following is NOT mentioned by the writer as a disadvantage of nesting boxes?

正确答案： B. They need to be maintained.

Q22, B

定位关键词：

disadvantage of nesting boxes


原文：

J段，“Nest box programmers help ease the shortage of nesting sites in some places, but there are not enough, they are expensive and they are not an adequate substitute by large, old trees, such as the habitat they represent and nectar, pollen and seeds they provide.”



解析：

题干要选择关于nesting boxes为提到的缺点，根据原文信息可知，巢箱编程器帮助缓解了一些地方筑巢地点的短缺，但数量还不够，价格昂贵，而且它们不能充分替代大型古树。所以B项：它们需要维护这一点是没有提到的。此题选B.


同义替换：

cost too much=expensive

Questions 23 - 26


Complete the summary below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER in each gap.

23

Parrots in Australia

There are 345 varieties of parrot in existence and, of these, live in Australia. As early as the , the mapmaker recognized that parrots lived in that part of the world. , the famous painter of animals and birds, commented on the size and beauty of the Australian parrot family.

正确答案： 23 one-sixth##1/6  |  24 16th century  |  25 Mercator  |  26 John Gould
Question 23

定位关键词：345

原文：

A段，“One-sixth of the world's 345 parrot species are found there”

解析：

根据原文可得六分之一的鹦鹉生活在澳大利亚，此空格填one-sixth或者1/6。

Question 24

定位关键词：mapmaker

原文：

B段，“In the 16th century, the German cartographer Mercator made a world map that included a place”

解析：

此空可预判需要填制图师发现鹦鹉生活在那个地方的时间，根据原文可知此处填16th century。

同义替换：

made a world map=mapmaker

Question 25

定位关键词：mapmaker

原文：

B段，“In the 16th century, the German cartographer Mercator made a world map that included a place”

解析：

此空可预判需要填制图师的名字，根据原文可知此处填Mercator。

同义替换：

made a world map=mapmaker

Question 26

定位关键词：painter

原文：

B段，“In 1865, the celebrated British naturalist and wildlife artist John Gould said: "No group of birds gives Australia so tropical and benign an air as the numerous species of this great family by which it is tenanted."

解析：

此空可预判需要填著名动物画家的名字，根据原文可知此处填John Gould，并且他说没有任何一种鸟类能像栖息在澳大利亚的这个大家族的众多物种那样，给澳大利亚带来如此热带和温和的气氛，这与题目中评论了澳大利亚鹦鹉家族的大小和美丽相符。

同义替换：

painter=artist
